problem: Circle $T$ has its center at point $T(-2,6)$. Circle $T$ is reflected across the $y$-axis and then translated 8 units down. What are the coordinates of the image of the center of circle $T$?

classified_questions.question: What is the center of circle T before any transformations?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What happens to the x-coordinate of the center when we reflect the circle across the y-axis?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the new x-coordinate of the center after reflecting across the y-axis?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What happens to the y-coordinate of the center when we translate the circle down?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we calculate the new y-coordinate of the center after translating down?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you determine the final coordinates of the image of the center of circle T based on the given transformations?
classified_questions.label: b
